claims.claim: The SEI film begins to decompose at approximately 90°C, exposing the anode and its lithium-embedded carbon components to the electrolyte, which then reacts exothermically with the electrolyte, causing the temperature to further rise.
claims.quote: As the temperature climbs to 90 ◦C, the SEI layer begins to disintegrate, exposing the anode and its lithium-embedded carbon components to the electrolyte, which then reacts exothermically with the electrolyte, causing the temperature to further rise

claims.claim: When the battery temperature exceeds 130°C, the diaphragm starts to melt and progressively fractures, and the interior of the battery is shorted by direct contact of the positive and negative electrodes.
claims.quote: When the temperature exceeds 130 ◦C, the diaphragm starts to melt and progressively fractures, and the interior of the battery is shorted by the direct contact of the positive and negative electrodes.

claims.claim: At temperatures up to 200°C, both the cathode and electrolyte start to break down and release significant amounts of toxic, dangerous, and combustible gases such as HF, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and hydrocarbons.
claims.quote: Up to 200 ◦C, both the cathode and electrolyte start to break down and even interact with the adhesive, releasing a significant amount of toxic, dangerous, and combustible gases, such as HF, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and hydrocarbons

claims.claim: In NCM and LFP cells, the release of flammable gas occurs approximately 1585 s and 987 s before the fire, respectively, indicating that gas signals provide earlier warning than temperature, voltage, and pressure signals.
claims.quote: Cui et al. [130] discovered that in NCM and LFP cells, the release of flammable gas occurs approximately 1585 s and 987 s before the fire, respectively. The results indicate that the unique gas signal can not only provide an early warning during thermal runaway but also provide an earlier warning than the temperature, voltage, and pressure signals.

claims.claim: When a gas signal is used as the benchmark for thermal runaway warning, an early warning can be given 7.05 min before thermal runaway peaks, compared to 5.96 min for voltage signals and only 0.5 min for temperature signals.
claims.quote: the figure indicates that when the gas signal is used as the benchmark, an early warning can be given 7.05 min before thermal runaway peaks, giving more time for escape and prevention, as opposed to when the voltage and temperature signals are used, which only allow for 5.96 min and 0.5 min of evacuation time, respectively.

claims.claim: For a battery at 100% SOC, the internal pressure buildup follows a power law as a function of time and reaches the critical pressure threshold of 1.5 MPa within 94 ms, after which the safety valve opens.
claims.quote: For a battery at 100 % SOC, this state reaches the critical pressure threshold of 1.5 MPa within only 94 ms. After that, the battery's safety valve opens through its outlet.

claims.claim: After the safety valve opens, the internal pressure drops sharply and approaches external atmospheric pressure within 0.03 ms.
claims.quote: Within 0.03 ms, the internal pressure approaches the external atmospheric pressure (Fig. 4(a)).

claims.claim: The pyrolysis gas jetting initiated at a maximum velocity of 450 m/s at the central outlet is completed in less than 2 milliseconds.
claims.quote: The initial peak velocity at the central outlet is 450 m/s, rising to the maximum value of about 500 m/s... This indicates that the initial jetting of the pyrolysis gas is completed in less than 2 milliseconds.

claims.claim: The initial jet fire lasts approximately 6 ms after safety valve rupture and lags behind the pyrolysis gas injection velocity due to accumulation of combustion heat.
claims.quote: The initial jet fire – which lasts around 6 ms after the safety valve rupture – lags behind the pyrolysis gas injection velocity and demonstrates pronounced downstream propagation.

claims.claim: At t = 94.05 ms, CO2 reaches its highest mass fraction of 70% above the safety valve nozzle during the initial jet fire combustion.
claims.quote: At this moment, CO2 exhibits the highest mass fraction of 70 %. Following this maximum, the mass fractions of combustible gases start to decrease gradually.

claims.claim: TR induced by slight overcharge cycles has a longer incubation period and involves heat accumulation, and once thermal runaway occurs, it poses greater hazards and spreads at a faster rate compared to TR induced by thermal or mechanical abuse.
claims.quote: In contrast with TR induced by thermal/mechanical abuse conditions, TR induced by slight overcharge cycles has a longer incubation period and involves a certain amount of heat accumulation. Once thermal runaway occurs, it poses greater hazards and spreads at a faster rate

claims.claim: When the SOC of a 1×5 NCM622 cell cluster was below 25%, no TR occurred within the cluster.
claims.quote: They found that when the SOC of the cell cluster was below 25 %, no TR occurred within the cluster.

claims.claim: In a 1×5 NCM622 cell cluster, total gas generation decreased from 17.48 mol to 6.17 mol when SOC was reduced from 100% to 50%.
claims.quote: the total gas generation decreased from 17.48 mol to 6.17 mol when the SOC was reduced from 100 % to 50 %

claims.claim: Using composite phase-change insulating materials completely inhibited thermal diffusion between lithium cells within a 1×4 cluster, achieving zero TRP within the component.
claims.quote: the use of composite phase-change insulating materials completely inhibited thermal diffusion between lithium cells within the cluster, achieving the goal of zero TRP within the component.

claims.claim: Compared to cells without insulation in a 1×4 LIB cluster, the average propagation time of TR increased by 5.27 times and 7.36 times for cells with different insulation materials.
claims.quote: Compared to cells without insulation, the average propagation time of TR increased by 5.27 times and 7.36 times for cells with different insulation materials.
